content.full_text:
RAVI MEHTA
CHAIRMAN

FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION

July 9, 1997

Gene Raper
Russo, Marsh & Raper, Inc.
770 L Street, Suite 950
Post Office Box 1863
Sacramento, California 95812

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. G-97-283

Dear Mr. Raper:

This letter is a response to your request for advice regarding the provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ Due to a lack of facts in your questions and their hypothetical
nature we will not provide you advice pursuant to Regulation 18329.

If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-
5660.

Sincerely,

Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel

By: Marte Castaños
Staff Counsel, Legal Division

SGC:MC:ak

¹ Government Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections 18109 -
18995, of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J STREET, SUITE 800, SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814 (916) 322-5660

sections.question_synthetic: The requester sought advice on provisions of the Political Reform Act, though the specific legal questions are not included in this declination letter.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declined to provide advice pursuant to Regulation 18329 due to lack of facts and the hypothetical nature of the questions posed.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a declination letter with no substantive question or conclusion sections. The actual questions from the requester are not included in the document provided. The letter references Regulation 18329 as the basis for declining to provide advice.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC declined to provide advice pursuant to Regulation 18329 due to lack of facts and the hypothetical nature of the questions posed.

embedding.summary: The FPPC declined to provide advice to Gene Raper regarding Political Reform Act provisions, citing insufficient factual detail and hypothetical nature of the inquiry. The FPPC invited the requester to submit additional questions for consideration.
